Empires and Puzzles Costumed Caitlin Review: The Justicar Crusader

Caitlin finally ditched the 'Wizard' robes for some heavy plate and a Fighter class. With the Justicar Crusader costume, she gets a massive stat injection, a nasty Bleed, and a mana engine that actually works. We break down why this costume takes her from zero to hero.
Costume Caitlin Review: The Crusader Returns
Costume Caitlin: The Slayer Redemption Arc
If the base version of Caitlin was a relic of the past,

is the Terminator sent from the future to save the Slayers family. Released in late 2024, this costume fixes almost every single issue the original had. It swaps the confused Wizard class for the clutch Fighter class, adds a massive Bleed DoT, and crucially, supercharges the Slayer mana generation stack. She is no longer just a utility piece; she is a stat monster.

Gone is the rugged, practical look of the base version. The Justicar Crusader dons a more ceremonial, golden armored look with a distinct "Joan of Arc" vibe. The visuals align with her new "Fighter" class—she looks ready to take a hit and keep coming back. The golden aesthetic fits the Holy element perfectly, making her look like a true champion of light (who occasionally makes enemies bleed out).
The difference is night and day.

These stats are fully competitive in the 2026 meta. She survives long enough for her Slayer stacks to matter.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Slayer family bonus (HP/Mana stacks) works in tandem with her special skill stacks. Combined, she becomes an engine of self-sustain. The costume makes this viable because she actually has the stats to survive until turn 10.
As mentioned, this is free damage. It effectively raises her special skill percentage significantly.
. Much better than the base version's Attack Up. Given her already high health, boosting her defense makes her incredibly difficult to remove from the board without a dedicated sniper.
The Slayers were fading into obscurity until this costume batch released. This update single-handedly revived the family's relevance for high-level play.
The costume essentially applies 3 years of stat updates in one go. She leaps from "useless" to "top-tier contender."
She is one of the best Yellow dispellers in the game. The combination of Dispel First + Bleed + Fighter Class + Self-Speedup is a potent package for both Offense and Defense (Flank/Wing).
Costume has ~40% higher stats across the board.
The +12% vs +5% stack difference is the primary reason to use the costume. It fixes the "Slayer Speed" issue.
> Wizard. Slayers are meant to stay alive to build stacks. Revive gives her a second chance to keep her stacks and fire again.
The costume is superior in every conceivable way.
Maximizes her already massive 2900+ HP pool and utilizes the Defense Up Aether power. Makes her an annoying wall that bleeds enemies dry.

Costume Caitlin is the apology letter from the developers for letting the Slayers fade away. She is robust, tactical, and punishing. While she lacks the instant gratification of a Fast sniper, her ability to dismantle the enemy's defenses while becoming faster and tankier every turn makes her a top-tier threat. She has reclaimed her spot in the light.
. A must-level if you own the costume.
